data.title: HBM: Record revenue, strong cash flow, and Copper World JV drive growth and financial strength
data.description:
Record 2025 results with $2.2B revenue and $1.1B adjusted EBITDA, strong free cash flow, and improved leverage. Copper World JV with Mitsubishi boosts project IRR and secures funding. 2026 guidance targets stable copper output, higher gold, and disciplined capital allocation.

Original document: Hudbay Minerals Inc [HBM] Slides Release — Feb. 20 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: Latvia Producer Prices Rise in January
data.description:
Producer prices in Latvia rose by 1.2% year-on-year in January 2026 from a downwardly revised 0.7% growth in the previous month.

Output increased in mining and quarrying (17.4% vs 17% in December 2025).

In addition, activity recovered for electricity, gas, steam, and air-conditioning supply (1% vs -3.3%).

Meanwhile, output growth slowed in water supply, sewerage, waste management, and remediation activities (5.2% vs 5.4%) and manufacturing (0.5% vs 0.9%), with notable decreases in food products (-0.7% vs 0.3%), beverages (3.2% vs 6.1%), paper and paper products (0.3% vs 2.6%), and chemical and chemical products (1.1% vs 1.8%).

On a monthly basis, output increased by 0.6% in January, rebounding from an upwardly revised 1.1% decline in the preceding period.

data.title: LUG: Record production, strong cash flow, and major reserve growth drive robust dividends and outlook
data.description:
Record gold production and financial results in 2025, with net income up 86% year-over-year and $1.0 billion in cash flow from operations. Significant reserve additions, robust exploration results, and a strong dividend payout highlight continued growth and shareholder returns.

Original document: Lundin Gold Inc. [LUG] Slides Release — Feb. 20 2026

data.title: Euro zone bond yields at multi-month lows as investors track ECB chatter, geopolitics
data.description:
Key points:
  • Lagarde plans to complete ECB term amid succession speculation
  • German 10-year yields decline for second week
  • Euro area bonds outperform US Treasuries

By Amanda Cooper and Stefano Rebaudo

Euro zone government bond yields headed for a second straight weekly decline on Friday, in a week marked by speculation over the leadership of the European Central Bank and by rising geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

ECB President Christine Lagarde told the Wall Street Journal on Thursday she expected to complete her eight-year term at the head of the central bank, following a report earlier in the week she was planning to step down.

That story, reported by the Financial Times on Wednesday, added impetus to the race to succeed Lagarde, bringing potential uncertainty to the ECB, which markets expect to hold interest rates at 2% for an extended period.

At the same time, investors are keeping a close eye on the Middle East, where a massive U.S. military buildup is fuelling fears of a wider war. President Donald Trump on Thursday warned Iran it must make a deal over its nuclear programme or "really bad things" will happen, while Tehran has threatened to retaliate against U.S. bases in the region if attacked, triggering a rise in the price of crude this week.

German 10-year yields (DE10YT=RR) were set for a decline of 2.5 basis points this week, which on the back of last week's 9-bp drop, brings the fall for February so far to more than 11 bps, the most since April last year.

The 10-year Bunds, a reference point for the wider euro zone market, were last yielding 2.73%, down around 1 bp on the day and just above this week's 2-1/2-month low of 2.725%.

Euro zone business activity accelerated faster than forecast this month.

EURO AREA BONDS OUTPACE US TREASURIES

Some analysts said Bunds have outperformed U.S. Treasuries in recent sessions, reflecting a view that German debt offers greater safety in the event of Iran‑related turmoil.

Yields on Italian BTPs and other euro zone bonds, including those of France and Spain are also hovering around multi‑month lows, having outpaced Treasuries too.

The benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yield traded at 4.07%, up from 4.018% hit earlier this week, the lowest level since November 28.

In the U.S., investors will get a first read of fourth-quarter gross domestic product, which is forecast to have expanded by 3%, and of the Federal Reserve's preferred measure of inflation, the core personal consumption expenditures price index.

Commerzbank strategists said they believed Bunds could come under some pressure, taking their cue from a touch of weakness in the U.S. Treasury market, after minutes from the Fed's last meeting showed policymakers were divided over the outlook for monetary policy, given the persistence of inflation.

"Everything considered, we suggest selling Bunds into strength this morning," Christoph Rieger, who is chief rates strategist at Commerzbank, said.

Italian 10-year yields were down 2 bps on the day at 3.344%, their lowest in four months, while French 10-year debt yields were also down nearly 1.5 bps at 3.302%, their lowest in over six months.

data.title: Nvidia Stock Rises. It's Nearing $30 Billion OpenAI Investment. Report Says. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Alex Kozul-Wright

Nvidia is reportedly close to agreeing a $30 billion investment into ChatGPT-developer OpenAI.

Nvidia shares were up 0.4% to $186.66 in premarket trading Friday. The stock has risen 2.8% over the past five trading sessions.

Among other chip makers, Advanced Micro Devices was up 0.3% while Broadcom rose 0.6% in premarket trading.

Nvidia, the leading maker of artificial-intelligence chips, is close to agreeing to invest $30 billion into OpenAI, according to the Financial Times, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

It would replace a commitment, announced in September, in which Nvidia said it would invest up to $100 billion in OpenAI to support the buildout of its AI data center capacity, according to the FT.

OpenAI didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from Barron's early on Friday. Nvidia declined to comment.

OpenAI is reportedly seeking $100 billion in new funding, which would value the ChatGPT-maker at roughly $830 billion. Much of the money raised would likely be spent on AI chips from Nvidia and its peers.

OpenAI is preparing for a public listing as soon as the fourth quarter of this year, according to The Wall Street Journal. A successful IPO of OpenAI would likely lift all AI-exposed stocks, including Nvidia.

Write to Alex Kozul-Wright at alexander.kozul-wright@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AITX's RAD Books Three More RIO Mini Units, Client Deployment Rises to Eight
data.description:
AITX announced RAD secured three additional RIO Mini units, bringing the client's total deployment to eight systems licensed with SARA.

Key Highlights:

  • RAD sold three additional RIO Mini units to a major consumer products manufacturer; installation scheduled in coming weeks.
  • The repeat order raises this client's total deployment to eight RIO Mini systems across multiple facilities, signaling rollout confidence.
  • Each new unit is licensed with SARA, increasing attachment rate and strengthening recurring revenue visibility.
  • Company completed SOC 2 Type 2 audit, validating internal controls and reinforcing credibility with enterprise and government clients.

Original SEC Filing: Artificial Intelligence Technology Solutions Inc. [ AITX ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Winpak Ltd expected to post earnings of 62 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Winpak Ltd is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on February 23 for the period ending December 31 2025

  • The Winnipeg Manitoba-based company is expected to report a 2.4% increase in revenue to $291.933 million from $285.14 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 3 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Winpak Ltd is for earnings of 62 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Winpak Ltd is C$48.00, about 6% below its last closing price of C$51.08
